title: keraselma leptophyllum
intro: Keraselma leptophyllum, also known as Keraselma, is a type of plant that belongs to the family of plants known as hepatics, or liverworts, which are small, non-vascular plants that grow in moist environments. Keraselma has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat conditions related to the kidneys and urinary system, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Keraselma include its potential to help with kidney stone prevention, inflammation reduction, and improved urinary tract health. Herbal preparations of Keraselma can be made by drying and crushing the plant, and it can be consumed as a tea or added to other herbal remedies to enhance their effects.
